([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Kementerian Kerja Raya*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): كمنترين كرجا راي‎), abbreviated **KKR**, is a [ministry](/wiki/Ministry_(government_department) "Ministry (government department)") of the [Government of Malaysia](/wiki/Government_of_Malaysia "Government of Malaysia") that is responsible for [public works](/wiki/Public_works "Public works"), [highway authority](/wiki/Highway_authority "Highway authority"), [construction industry](/wiki/Construction_industry "Construction industry"), [engineers](/wiki/Engineers "Engineers"), [architects](/wiki/Architects "Architects") and [quantity surveyors](/wiki/Quantity_surveyor "Quantity surveyor").

["Hasnol Zam Zam appointed as Works Ministry sec-gen"](https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2023/04/04/hasnol-zam-zam-appointed-as-works-ministry-sec-gen). *The Star*. 4 April 2023. Retrieved 4 April 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Government ministry of Malaysia

Ministry of Works

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *Kementerian Kerja Raya*   كمنترين كرجا راي‎ (KKR) | |

| Headquarters of Ministry of Works | |

| Formed | 31 August 1957; 68 years ago (1957-08-31) |
| Preceding Ministry | * [Ministry of Works and Public Amenities](/wiki/Ministry_of_Works_and_Public_Amenities_(Malaysia) "Ministry of Works and Public Amenities (Malaysia)") |
| Jurisdiction | [Government of Malaysia](/wiki/Government_of_Malaysia "Government of Malaysia") |
| Headquarters | Kompleks Kerja Raya, [Jalan Sultan Salahuddin](/wiki/Jalan_Sultan_Salahuddin "Jalan Sultan Salahuddin"), 50580 [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur") |
| Motto | Towards the People's Prosperity (*Ke Arah Kesejahteraan Rakyat*) |

| Ministry executives | * Dato' Seri Azman bin Ibrahim[[1]](#cite_note-1), Secretary-General * Datuk Dr Khairus Masnan bin Abdul Khalid, Deputy Secretary-General (Policy and Development) * Dato' Zainal Alhakab bin Seman, Deputy Secretary-General (Management) |

| [Ministry of Works](https://www.facebook.com/KementerianKerjaRaya) on [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook_(identifier) "Facebook (identifier)") | |

The **Ministry of Works** ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Kementerian Kerja Raya*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): كمنترين كرجا راي‎), abbreviated **KKR**, is a [ministry](/wiki/Ministry_(government_department) "Ministry (government department)") of the [Government of Malaysia](/wiki/Government_of_Malaysia "Government of Malaysia") that is responsible for [public works](/wiki/Public_works "Public works"), [highway authority](/wiki/Highway_authority "Highway authority"), [construction industry](/wiki/Construction_industry "Construction industry"), [engineers](/wiki/Engineers "Engineers"), [architects](/wiki/Architects "Architects") and [quantity surveyors](/wiki/Quantity_surveyor "Quantity surveyor").

## Organisation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Works_(Malaysia)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Organisation")]

* Minister of Works
  + Deputy Minister of Works
    - Secretary-General
      * Under the Authority of Secretary-General
        + Legal Advisor's Office
        + Internal Audit Unit
        + Integrity Unit
        + Corporate Communication Unit
      * Deputy Secretary-General (Policy and Development)
        + Highway Planning Division
        + Policy and International Division
        + Development and Privatization Division
        + Facilities Management Division
      * Deputy Secretary-General (Management)
        + Human Resources Management Division
        + Management Services Division
        + Account Division
        + Finance Division
        + Corporate Planning Division
        + Information Management Division

### Federal department

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Works_(Malaysia)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Federal department")]

1. [Malaysian Public Works Department](/wiki/Malaysian_Public_Works_Department "Malaysian Public Works Department"), or *Jabatan Kerja Raya Malaysia* (JKR). ([Official site](https://www.jkr.gov.my/))

### Federal agencies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Works_(Malaysia)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Federal agencies")]

1. [Malaysian Highway Authority](/wiki/Malaysian_Highway_Authority "Malaysian Highway Authority"), or *Lembaga Lebuhraya Malaysia* (LLM). ([Official site](http://www.llm.gov.my/))
2. Construction Industry Development Board Malaysia (CIDB), or *Lembaga Pembangunan Industri Pembinaan Malaysia*. ([Official site](https://www.cidb.gov.my/))
3. [Board of Engineers Malaysia](/wiki/Board_of_Engineers_Malaysia "Board of Engineers Malaysia") (BEM), or *Lembaga Jurutera Malaysia*. ([Official site](http://www.bem.org.my/))
4. Board of Architects Malaysia, or *Lembaga Arkitek Malaysia* (LAM). ([Official site](https://www.lam.gov.my/))
5. Board of Quantity Surveyors Malaysia (BQSM), or *Lembaga Juruukur Bahan Malaysia*. ([Official site](http://www.bqsm.gov.my/))

## Key legislation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Works_(Malaysia)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Key legislation")]

The Ministry of Works is responsible for administration of several key [acts of parliament](/wiki/List_of_Acts_of_Parliament_in_Malaysia "List of Acts of Parliament in Malaysia").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Works_(Malaysia)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: History")]

In 1954, the British government took several measures to separate the administration of the Malay Peninsula (Malaya) from its main administration centralised in Singapore. This marked the beginning point where the local government departments in the Malay Peninsula were permitted to implement their own policies and programs respectively. In 1956, the chief minister and several ministers were appointed to lead the Federation of Malay Peninsula.

In the same year, several ministries were formed including the Ministry of Works, which then was originally named the Ministry of Works, Post and Telecom. Sardon bin Haji Jubir was the first minister to lead this ministry. The functions and responsibilities of the Public Works Department were retained and put under the purview of the ministry’s administration. In 1957, the ministry was reorganised and renamed the Ministry of Works and Transportation.

The rapid progress made in the country's development and socioeconomic condition during the 1970s resulted in the increase of the ministry’s functions and roles. With the addition of new roles, the Ministry was renamed once more to the Ministry of Works and Public Amenities in 1978. However, in line with the specialisation of responsibilities, the government renamed the ministry as the Ministry of Works Malaysia in the 1980s.
